title: Content Delivery Networks (CDNs) Quiz
description: Test your knowledge about the critical role of CDNs and content delivery in modern web applications.

questions.question: What is the primary goal of a 'Content Delivery Network' (CDN)?
options: To store all of the application's users.
To reduce latency and improve performance by distributing content spatially relative to end users.
To automatically generate a backup of the data.
To hide the application's IP address.

questions.question: Which of these is NOT typically served via a CDN?
What is 'Cache Busting' in the context of CDNs?
options: A way to encrypt files using a secret key.
Adding a unique hash or version number to the filename (e.g., style.v2.css) so that the CDN is forced to fetch the fresh version when the filename changes.
A way to automatically compress files.

questions.question: Which of these is a popular CDN provider famous for its free tier and DDoS protection?
What's the primary benefit of 'Edge Caching' in a CDN?
options: Content (like entire HTML pages) can be cached on a server very close to the user, drastically reducing response time.
To automatically generate a backup of the data.
